---
title: "Vercel Blob Agent"
description: "Template demonstrating file storage and retrieval using Vercel Blob with upload, list, and delete tools"
---

<Info>
  **Source:**
  [`examples/agent-with-vercel-blob`](https://github.com/AgentlyHQ/aixyz/tree/main/examples/agent-with-vercel-blob)
</Info>

## Overview

This template shows how to integrate [Vercel Blob](https://vercel.com/docs/vercel-blob) storage into an aixyz agent. The agent can upload text content, list stored files, and delete blobs — all via natural language requests gated behind x402 micropayments.

## Project Structure

```
agent-with-vercel-blob/
├── aixyz.config.ts         # Agent metadata and skills
├── app/
│   ├── agent.ts            # Agent definition with blob storage tools
│   ├── tools/
│   │   ├── uploadBlob.ts   # Upload text content to Vercel Blob
│   │   ├── listBlobs.ts    # List stored blobs with metadata
│   │   └── deleteBlob.ts   # Delete a blob by URL
│   └── icon.png
├── .env.example            # Required environment variables
├── package.json
└── vercel.json
```

## Skills

| Skill       | Description                                              |
| ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| Upload File | Upload text content to Vercel Blob and receive a URL     |
| List Files  | List all stored blobs with URLs, sizes, and upload dates |
| Delete File | Remove a blob from storage by its URL                    |

## Configuration

```typescript
const config: AixyzConfig = {
  name: "Vercel Blob Agent",
  description: "An AI agent that stores, retrieves, and manages files using Vercel Blob storage.",
  version: "0.1.0",
  x402: {
    payTo: "0x...",
    network: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production" ? "eip155:8453" : "eip155:84532",
  },
  skills: [
    { id: "upload-blob", name: "Upload File", ... },
    { id: "list-blobs",  name: "List Files",  ... },
    { id: "delete-blob", name: "Delete File", ... },
  ],
};
```

## Tool Pattern

Each tool imports directly from `@vercel/blob`. The token is read automatically from the `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` environment variable.

```typescript
// app/tools/uploadBlob.ts
import { put } from "@vercel/blob";
import { tool } from "ai";
import { z } from "zod";

export default tool({
  description: "Upload text content to Vercel Blob storage. Returns the public URL of the stored file.",
  inputSchema: z.object({
    pathname: z.string().min(1).describe("The filename/path for the blob, e.g. 'notes/hello.txt'."),
    content: z.string().min(1).describe("The text content to upload."),
  }),
  execute: async ({ pathname, content }) => {
    const blob = await put(pathname, content, { access: "public", addRandomSuffix: true });
    return { url: blob.url, pathname: blob.pathname, contentType: blob.contentType, size: blob.size };
  },
});
```

## Setting Up Vercel Blob

1. Go to your [Vercel dashboard](https://vercel.com/dashboard)
2. Navigate to **Storage** → **Create** → **Blob**
3. Copy the `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` from the `.env.local` tab into your local `.env.local`

When deploying to Vercel, link the Blob store to your project so the token is automatically injected.

## Environment Variables

| Variable                | Description                                          |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY`        | OpenAI API key                                       |
| `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` | Vercel Blob read/write token (from Vercel dashboard) |

## Payment

The agent charges `$0.001` per request via x402 on Base (mainnet in production, Base Sepolia in development).

## Running

```bash
cd examples/agent-with-vercel-blob
bun install
bun run dev
```

Endpoints available at `http://localhost:3000`:

| Endpoint                       | Protocol | Description           |
| ------------------------------ | -------- | --------------------- |
| `/.well-known/agent-card.json` | A2A      | Agent discovery card  |
| `/agent`                       | A2A      | JSON-RPC task handler |
| `/mcp`                         | MCP      | Tool endpoint         |
